calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-25-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Scott Poling appeared by zoom for Maryann Valentine (Trustee).
Bruce Feder (Court Appointed Counsel for Samuel Bell) appeared by zoom.

The Court stated that it reviewed everything and that it does not see any issues in the accounting, or in the compensation request. Mr. Poling and Mr. Feder submit on those matters. The Court noted that Court approval of the accountings isn't required and that the petition seeks to waive accountings, and pointed out that if no approval is required, there is nothing to waive. Mr. Poling stated that there is the issue of exonerating the bond, that the accountings were set when the prior accounting orders were signed, and that the Trustee will continue to provide accountings, but not Court accountings. The Court stated that bond was imposed when the trust was established and has continued, and that it still provides a measure of security and allows the Court jurisdiction should there be a need for any issues to be addressed to the Court. The Court also stated that the cost of the bond premium is relatively low and that if there is an issue of the bond being lowered, the premium could be lowered. Mr. Poling stated that he agrees with the Court and that the amount of the bond is relative to the last accounting. Mr. Feder stated that Mr. bell is doing well, that they had a nice conversation yesterday, that Mr. Bell is in a training program, that Mr. Bell has goals and that he is hoping to get some employment. Mr. Feder also stated that Mr. Bell is taking classes, that Mr. Bell is optimistic about his future and that Mr. Bell's main concern is about housing in the future. The Second Account of Trustee and Petition for Compensation, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
